business_name.description: Name of the customer business the voice co-worker represents.

escalation_policy.description: When and how the co-worker should recommend human escalation.
escalation_policy.example: Escalate billing disputes, account security issues, medical emergencies, and any answer not grounded in the knowledge base.

knowledge_text.description: Editable customer-service knowledge used as the initial RAG text for this run.
knowledge_text.example: Hours: Monday-Friday 9 AM-5 PM. Emergency calls are escalated to the on-call dispatcher.

opening_message.description: First spoken message after the caller connects.
opening_message.example: Thanks for calling Acme Home Services. How can I help today?

service_scope.description: Plain-language scope of what the co-worker should help customers with.
service_scope.example: Answer questions about appointments, service areas, pricing basics, and support handoff.

spark_host.description: SSH destination for the NVIDIA Spark box.

voice.description: TTS voice name or preset used by the Magpie service.

voice_https_port.description: HTTPS port for the Spark-hosted WebRTC voice page.

contract.summary: Runs a Spark-hosted HTTPS/WebRTC customer-service voice co-worker backed by NVIDIA ASR, Nemotron vLLM, Magpie TTS, and editable plain-text RAG knowledge.
users: Support teams, customer operations leads, small businesses, service desks, and AI operations teams.

description: Runs a Spark-hosted HTTPS/WebRTC customer-service voice co-worker backed by NVIDIA ASR, Nemotron vLLM, Magpie TTS, and editable plain-text RAG knowledge.

steps.goal: Validate Spark, prepare run knowledge, and make the NVIDIA voice stack ready.
steps.label: Prepare Voice Service

steps.goal: Run the Spark-hosted WebRTC page so a customer can talk to the co-worker.
steps.label: Serve Customer Conversation

metadata.agent_role: Customer-service voice co-worker that answers from the customer knowledge base, asks clarifying questions when needed, and recommends escalation when it is unsure or outside scope.
metadata.benefit: Lets an operator launch a local OtterDesk run, update plain-text support knowledge, and hand customers a Spark HTTPS voice link backed by the NVIDIA stack.
metadata.customizable_for: Customer support, service desks, appointment intake, SaaS help desks, retail support, and internal help lines.
metadata.description: Runs a Spark-hosted HTTPS/WebRTC customer-service voice co-worker backed by NVIDIA ASR, Nemotron vLLM, Magpie TTS, and editable plain-text RAG knowledge.

human_control.reason: The co-worker can converse with customers and recommend escalation, but it does not approve refunds, account changes, or other business actions by itself.

fields.description: The name the voice co-worker should use with callers.
fields.label: Business name

fields.default: Answer common support questions and recommend escalation when unsure.
fields.description: The boundaries of what the co-worker may discuss.
fields.label: Service scope

fields.description: Plain text support knowledge seeded into the run and editable from the voice page.
fields.label: Knowledge text

fields.default: Escalate billing disputes, emergencies, account security issues, and any answer not grounded in knowledge.
fields.description: Rules for asking a human to take over.
fields.label: Escalation policy

fields.description: SSH destination for the NVIDIA box used by pre-launch and cleanup.
fields.label: Spark SSH host

fields.description: HTTPS/WebRTC voice page port on Spark.
fields.label: Spark HTTPS port

init_config_review.instruction: When running in OtterDesk, show these overwriteable fields during init config and ask the user to confirm or edit them before launch.

input_contract.description: Runs a Spark-hosted HTTPS/WebRTC customer-service voice co-worker backed by NVIDIA ASR, Nemotron vLLM, Magpie TTS, and editable plain-text RAG knowledge.

optional_inputs.description: TTS voice name or preset used by the Magpie service.
optional_inputs.name: voice

optional_inputs.description: SSH destination for the NVIDIA Spark box.
optional_inputs.name: spark_host

optional_inputs.description: HTTPS port for the Spark-hosted WebRTC voice page.
optional_inputs.name: voice_https_port

file.description: JSON object loaded from a local file path.

json.description: Inline business and knowledge settings supplied through config or runtime overrides.

live_voice.description: Runtime WebRTC voice page served from Spark.

required_inputs.description: Name of the customer business the voice co-worker represents.
required_inputs.name: business_name

required_inputs.description: Plain-language scope of what the co-worker should help customers with.
required_inputs.example: Answer questions about appointments, service areas, pricing basics, and support handoff.
required_inputs.name: service_scope

required_inputs.description: First spoken message after the caller connects.
required_inputs.example: Thanks for calling Acme Home Services. How can I help today?
required_inputs.name: opening_message

required_inputs.description: Editable customer-service knowledge used as the initial RAG text for this run.
required_inputs.example: Hours: Monday-Friday 9 AM-5 PM. Emergency calls are escalated to the on-call dispatcher.
required_inputs.name: knowledge_text

required_inputs.description: When and how the co-worker should recommend human escalation.
required_inputs.example: Escalate billing disputes, account security issues, medical emergencies, and any answer not grounded in the knowledge base.
required_inputs.name: escalation_policy

validation_rules.message: spark_host must point to the Spark SSH host.
validation_rules.name: spark_host_present

validation_rules.message: voice_https_port must be a user-accessible HTTPS port.
validation_rules.name: voice_https_port_number

artifacts.description: Stable run-store files and domain-specific outputs.

human.description: Human notices, approval requests, responses, timeouts, and applied decisions.

input_skills.description: Connector-backed or reusable skill-backed input sources.

inputs.description: Resolved runtime inputs and adapter provenance.

logs.description: Runtime stdout/stderr and explicit log artifacts.

output_skills.description: Optional fan-out destinations after local run-store writes.

outputs.description: Primary result and final user-facing artifact.

resources.description: Resource samples such as token, runtime, connector, and disk usage.

streams.description: Named data or event streams exposed by the blueprint.

llm.agent_role: Customer-service voice co-worker that answers from the customer knowledge base, asks clarifying questions when needed, and recommends escalation when it is unsure or outside scope.

metadata.llm_agent_role: Customer-service voice co-worker that answers from the customer knowledge base, asks clarifying questions when needed, and recommends escalation when it is unsure or outside scope.
metadata.name: Generic Customer Service Voice AI Co-worker
metadata.output: Visual detection observations with count, label, category, color, position, activity, confidence, and alert status.

final_artifact.description: The durable user-facing artifact should summarize the result, recommendation, confidence, evidence, next steps, and source references.

output_contract.stdout: single JSON object, event envelope, or bundle path

metadata.problem_solved: Teams need a reusable voice co-worker that can answer customer questions from editable local knowledge without building a one-off demo for each business.

quick_test.description: Quick test mode shortens the sample interval, disables Slack, and uses mock visual detection unless OLLAMA_LIVE_IN_QUICK_TEST=1.

metadata.simulation_type: Spark-hosted real-time WebRTC voice conversation with editable text RAG.

standard.execution_model: load_metadata -> resolve_config -> load_inputs -> start_run_store -> observe_simulation_state -> call_llm_agent -> evaluate_workflow_state -> route_by_context -> request_human_input -> apply_decision_to_simulation -> emit_events -> write_final_artifact -> fan_out_output_skills

metadata.target_user: Video operators, security reviewers, safety operations teams, and site-access coordinators.
metadata.target_users: Support teams, customer operations leads, small businesses, service desks, and AI operations teams.

video_source.description: Default mapped RTSP endpoint consumed by the OpenShell worker. The host-side pre-launch hook loops the bundled demo video into the selected local RTSP port before launch.

web_ui.description: Local shared OtterDesk dashboard with Spark voice URL, service health, events, knowledge metadata, and conversation artifacts.

name: Generic Customer Service Voice AI Co-worker

workers.role: Prepare Spark voice runtime and knowledge store

workers.model: NVIDIA ASR + Nemotron vLLM + Magpie TTS
workers.role: Host WebRTC voice conversation service on Spark

primary.role: Customer-service voice co-worker that answers from the customer knowledge base, asks clarifying questions when needed, and recommends escalation when it is unsure or outside scope.
